title: Timestamps are unvalidated strings and the inbox --since cursor is lossy at second granularity
description: Audit finding (MED). created_at/claimed_at etc. are z.string() with no ISO-8601 constraint, yet ordering and the inbox --since cursor rely on lexical order equaling chronological order (ORDER BY created_at; m.created_at <= since). A non-ISO or non-uniform-offset timestamp sorts/paginates wrongly. Separately, because created_at is second-resolution, using a message's created_at as --since silently drops other messages from that same second (cursor by timestamp alone is lossy).
expected: Timestamps validate as ISO-8601; cursoring never skips unseen messages.
actual: Any string passes; same-second messages can be skipped by --since.
next_steps: Constrain timestamp fields to ISO-8601 in zod (datetime with offset).
Make the inbox cursor compound (created_at + id) so same-second messages are not lost.
